# Positioning Frame

Andrew Shipley built the portfolio below as an unpaid hobby while running a law practice. The throughline is not "interested in AI" — it is operational judgment applied to production systems across multiple verticals. The right role is one where a named capability gap is the structural problem and his shape is the unlock. That role needs him more than he needs it. Orpheus exists to find those roles, surface the asymmetry, and write on his behalf from a position of evaluating the opportunity — not applying for it.

**The Rhodes Scholar credential is structural, not decorative.** It is not a prestige signal in the same category as Yale Law or Gunderson. It is a different category of claim: a selection committee of senior people looked at this candidate at 21 or 22, running a process specifically designed to identify people expected to be consequential in their domains, and placed a bet. A hiring committee that understands the credential reads it as: "identified at 21 by a selection process designed to find people of judgment and consequence, and now here demonstrating the full arc." This changes the starting position of every conversation at institutions that understand it. The Rhodes pre-licenses the evaluator register — content generated by Orpheus should default to the evaluator voice rather than softening toward applicant hedging.

## Active Identities

- **operator**: Chief of Staff, Chief of Staff to CEO, Chief of Staff to Managing Partner …
- **legal**: General Counsel, Head of Legal, VP Legal …
- **research**: Head of Research Operations, Director of Research Programs, Research Operations, Program Officer …
- **applied_ai_operator**: Head of AI, Head of Applied AI, Director of AI …

## Portfolio

- **NLSAFE**: Rust-based verifiable build infrastructure for safety-critical AI systems — LLVM IR static scanner for unsafe memory patterns, MLIR dialect-aware audit tool for dynamic ops and layout violations, Bazel BEP → SLSA provenance transformer (Apache 2.0). identity_boosts: research, operator, applied_ai_operator.

- **first-agent**: Production AI system deployed for Tre Borden /Co. Lead Finder (Claude + server-side web_search, SSE streaming, ICP 1-10 scoring) and 947-line Permit Intelligence engine (LA/NYC/SF via Socrata connectors, 120+ owner patterns, 275 passing tests). Flask + gunicorn on Railway. identity_boosts: applied_ai_operator, operator, research.

- **charlie**: Autonomous multi-agent intelligence system deployed on Railway for Liz Varner covering the entertainment industry. Four agents: Ingestion, Analysis, Brief (Signal / Bullshit Flag / Your World), Thesis. Per-client persistent context layer. identity_boosts: applied_ai_operator, operator, research.

- **mrkt**: Empirical research pipeline on public-company M&A agreements. Four Anthropic tool_use schemas, 152-agreement MAUD corpus, 99.7% extraction success, 91-94% expert-label agreement, OLS with HC1 robust SEs (stdlib-only, deliberate auditability). identity_boosts: legal, research, applied_ai_operator.

- **Orpheus**: Self-search engine on MCP architecture with four-identity ranker, behavioral feedback loop, Pylon observability layer, compound-fit scoring, asymmetry detection. identity_boosts: applied_ai_operator, operator, research.

- **CW_Actual**: Single-file, canvas-rendered park-management simulation adapted from the George Saunders story — ~4,400 lines, no framework, no dependencies, four-checkpoint moral ledger, in-browser hotspot authoring tool. identity_boosts: applied_ai_operator, operator.

## Credentials

Juris Doctor in Law, Yale Law School · Rhodes Scholar; DPhil in Experimental Psychology, University of Oxford (co-authors: John T. Jost, NYU; William H. Dutton, Oxford Internet Institute) · Fulbright Scholar in Cross-Cultural Psychology, University of Wellington

## Positioning Thesis

### The Rhodes as structural signal

The Rhodes is not a credential line item. It is a permanent override on pattern-matching at institutions that understand it — which are disproportionately the institutions worth targeting: foundations, policy organizations, frontier AI labs, senior roles in government and law, and media companies with intellectual cultures.

What the credential actually claims: a selection committee of senior people evaluated this candidate at 21 or 22 and bet that he would be consequential. Not successful — consequential. In a domain that matters. The selection criteria include literary and scholastic ability, energy to use talents to the full, truth and courage, devotion to duty, protection of the weak, kindness, and — the operative one — moral force of character combined with instincts to lead. In practice, committees are identifying people whose judgment will shape something. That is a fundamentally different claim than "smart and credentialed."

Because the Rhodes cohort is roughly 32 Americans per year and is actively networked — Rhodes House, American Rhodes Scholars network, cohort-year ties, ongoing programming — it functions as permanent membership in a specific tier of people who are assumed to be capable of consequential work until proven otherwise. For hiring committees at the organizations worth targeting, seeing Rhodes doesn't prompt "oh, prestigious" — it prompts "identified at 21 as someone expected to matter, and now here showing what happened." The conversation starts there.

**The evaluator register is pre-licensed.** An application that opens with a diagnosis of the company's problem — stating what they need and why this profile addresses it — does not read as arrogant or presumptuous for a candidate with this arc. It reads as calibrated. Content generators should use the evaluator voice as the default, not as a stylistic exception.

### The DPhil as distinct credential

The Rhodes got to Oxford. The DPhil is what happened there. They are not the same thing.

The DPhil in Experimental Psychology at Oxford is a real research credential: three peer-reviewed publications across three domains with named collaborators. Psychological Reports (2008, sole author): social comparison and prosocial behavior. Reuters Institute / Oxford Internet Institute volume (2010, co-author with William H. Dutton): political engagement and televised debates — published in the same year as the first UK televised leadership debates, making it historically load-bearing in a way that is easy to cite. PLOS ONE (2020, co-author with John T. Jost at NYU): moral foundations as the product of motivated social cognition — the Jost co-authorship is a named signal to anyone in academic social psychology or political psychology.

The publications are not decorative. They are evidence of ability to do real empirical work and have it reviewed by domain experts. For program officer roles at foundations, senior fellow searches, and research operations roles, this is the primary qualifying credential. The legal and operating track record is secondary for these roles — it is proof of ability to execute, not the basis for the conversation.

### The arc and the roles it unlocks

The right frame is not "what role can this profile land?" It is "which roles exist specifically because this combination is rare?" The combination is:

- Empirical social scientist (real DPhil, real publications, real collaborators)
- Yale JD and corporate law practice at Gunderson-level VC firm (Gunderson ranked #1 global VC law firm by Pitchbook)
- Co-founding partner of a boutique serving 100+ startups, $250M+ in strategic transactions
- Chief of Staff to a quantum computing startup CEO, promoted from outside counsel
- Director of Operations at a Series A AI infrastructure company (10x ARR, SOC II, ARIA safety grant)
- Five shipped production AI systems as an unpaid hobby, two deployed for named clients in named verticals

That combination exists once per decade in a hiring pool, if that. The roles that fit it are not generic senior operator roles or generic GC roles. They are roles where the company has a problem that looks like: "we need someone who has operated at the level of a CoS/GC, who understands AI infrastructure well enough to evaluate it, who has research credibility to navigate the academic and policy landscape, and who is not going to be intimidated by the complexity of the problem." There are not many of those roles. Finding them is what Orpheus is for.

### Anti-patterns and calibration

Applying below the right seniority level is the main anti-pattern. The profile reads as overqualified at mid-level, and that overqualification creates hiring committee hesitation that no cover letter resolves. If there is a consistent rejection pattern from a particular tier of roles, that is a calibration signal about where to aim, not about how to adjust the application.

The Rhodes network is a practical warm-intro channel that the current system treats as zero signal. Program officer pipelines at major foundations, senior fellow searches, and research operations roles are disproportionately staffed by people who understand the credential and who can provide warm intros or contextual reads. Using the network deliberately is different from credential-dropping in applications.

The thrilling moment — the role worth taking — is the one where a company or organization has a problem they cannot easily define, a hiring committee sophisticated enough to read the full arc, and a mandate wide enough that the profile is the unlock rather than one of many candidates. The asymmetry filter in Orpheus is designed to find these. The evaluator voice in the content generators is designed to write for them.
